gdpr_reasons: Visitor tracking: Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, Google Tag Manager for WordPress:1.20.3

findings.risk: The browser has no restrictions on which scripts can run on the page. If the site is compromised, injected scripts can operate without constraint.
The website can be embedded in frames on other sites. This enables clickjacking attacks where users interact with hidden elements overlaid on the legitimate page.
Browsers may misinterpret uploaded files as executable content. This is primarily relevant if the site accepts file uploads.
The browser is not instructed to restrict access to device features (camera, microphone, geolocation). Third-party scripts embedded on the page could request these permissions.
The browser may send the full page URL to third-party sites when visitors click links. This can leak internal page paths or query parameters.
The exact WordPress version is visible to anyone viewing the page source. This allows attackers to look up known vulnerabilities specific to this version and target them directly.

findings.description: Data-handling plugins detected: Woocommerce, Contact Form 7
findings.risk: These plugins collect or process user data (form submissions, bookings, payments). If the site or plugin has a vulnerability, this data could be exposed. Keeping these plugins updated is critical for GDPR compliance.
Each plugin is additional code from a third-party developer. Outdated or abandoned plugins are a common entry point for attackers. Plugins should be reviewed and kept updated.

The server advertises which backend technologies it runs. This gives attackers information about which exploits may be applicable. It does not mean the site is vulnerable, but it reduces the effort needed to find an attack vector.

findings.description: WordPress themes detected: Minimalish, Oxygen Is Not A Theme
findings.risk: The active theme is visible in the HTML source. Outdated themes are a common source of vulnerabilities, similar to plugins.
The installed version of Contact Form 7 is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.
The installed version of Woocommerce is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.
The installed version of Content Protector is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.
The installed version of Trustpilot Reviews is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.
The installed version of Lightbox Popup is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.
